LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-02-2023 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41. PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY JUNE 02, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

The matter is discussed. The Court announces it did not receive any contest email, there is no appearance by the opposing side, therefore the matter will not be called. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS' MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended COMPLAINT.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Douglas O'Connor, Esq. 415-583-1002 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

The matter is discussed. The Court announces it did not receive any contest email, there is no appearance by the opposing side, therefore the matter will not be called. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT.

Sustained without leave to amend.

There are 13 causes of action in the operative second amended complaint (SAC), which is based on plaintiff having been circumsized shortly after his birth, with parental consent. (Apparently the SAC alleges the consent was procured by fraud, although it isn't clear what the fraud was.)

The statute of limitations has run on all the causes of action in the SAC. The events took place around the time of plaintiff's birth in 1977, and he was aware of the situation and asserts he had suffered injury at least by the time he was an adolescent, as the SAC alleges. SAC paragraphs 104, 110. The longest limitations period for the causes of action is four years, which expired long ago.

Plaintiff treats his claims as brought under the state's Childhood Sexual Assault Act, CCP 340.1. See generally, Coats v. New Haven Unified School District (2020) 46 Cal.App.5th 415. The longest period of time he normally would have under the extended limitations period there is 22 years from the date plaintiff reached 18. Born in 1977, he reached 18 in 1995. (As the SAC shows, he discovered his "psychological injury" in adolescence, so the alternative limitations period does not assist plaintiff.) The complaint wasn't filed until 2022. However the "look back" window created by AB 218 (enacted October 14, 2019) permitted claims to be filed through December 31, 2022, which thus includes plaintiff's claim.

However, claims under the Childhood Sexual Assault Act require certificates of merit from a lawyer and mental health practitioner. Coats, 46 Cal.App.5th at 432. There are none. Plaintiff's opposition concedes this. Thus even treated as a claim under the Act, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CK)

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to strike is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 435.5, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a motion to strike is filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by June 02, 2023
